introduction: Essential Part questions ask which component is necessary for an object or idea to exist as named. You must distinguish what is required from what is merely common, useful, or related.
explanation:
CONCEPT

An essential part is a necessary feature: without it, the thing cannot be what it is called. A book needs pages (or a readable medium); a triangle needs three sides. Non-essential items are frequent companions (a bookmark with a book) but not definitional.

HOW TO SOLVE

1) Define the word in the simplest accurate way.
2) Ask: If I remove this part, can the thing still exist under that name?
3) Reject options that are optional accessories, materials that can vary, or related objects.
4) Prefer the feature that is true in all normal cases of the concept.

IMPORTANT RULES

• Essential ≠ most common. Shoes often have laces, but laces are not essential to "shoe".
• Essential ≠ purpose alone if the stem asks for a part/component.
• Abstract nouns: essential part may be a defining quality (e.g. risk for "gamble").
• Object relationship variants link a whole to a necessary piece.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Choosing a popular accessory instead of a defining part.
2) Confusing material (wood, metal) with structure.
3) Picking a synonym of the whole object.
4) Over-narrowing (requiring a brand or modern feature).
5) Ignoring abstract essentials for idea-based stems.

formulas.title: Necessity test
formulas.content: X is essential to Y if Y cannot be Y without X

formulas.title: Accessory filter
formulas.content: If X can be absent and Y still remains Y, X is not essential

formulas.title: Definition anchor
formulas.content: Start from a one-line dictionary-style definition of Y

shortcutTips.title: Remove it test
shortcutTips.tip: Mentally delete the option; if the object still makes sense, reject it.

shortcutTips.title: Avoid materials
shortcutTips.tip: Wood/metal/plastic are rarely essential unless the object is defined by that material.

shortcutTips.title: Avoid extras
shortcutTips.tip: Covers, cases, decorations, and optional tools are usually wrong.

shortcutTips.title: Think minimal
shortcutTips.tip: The essential part is the smallest necessary piece of the definition.

solvedExamples.problem: Essential part of Book: (A) Pictures (B) Pages (C) Learning (D) Eraser
solvedExamples.solution: Pages — a book is a bound set of pages (or equivalent readable sheets); pictures and learning are optional; eraser is unrelated.
steps: Definition: a book consists of written/printed pages bound together.
Without pages (or a page-like medium), it is not a book.
Pictures/learning may be present but are not required.

solvedExamples.problem: Essential part of Shoes: (A) Laces (B) Sole (C) Polish (D) Socks
solvedExamples.solution: Sole — footwear needs a sole; laces/polish/socks are optional.
steps: A shoe must have a bottom that contacts the ground.
Laces are absent in many shoes.
Polish and socks are not parts of the shoe itself.

solvedExamples.problem: Essential part of Triangle: (A) Area (B) Angle (C) Three sides (D) Color
solvedExamples.solution: Three sides — by definition a triangle is a three-sided polygon.
steps: Definition requires three sides (and three angles).
Among options, three sides is the clearest necessary structural part.
Area and color are not required to be a triangle; "angle" alone is incomplete versus three sides.
